content.full_text:
VERNE G. SANDERS                    ACCOUNTANCY CORPORATION

VERNE G. SANDERS, JR. CPA
BECKY L. BELGRAM, CPA
KENNETH L. HAMILTON, CPA
DAVID A. PALM, CPA

February 2, 1991


Ms. Carla Wardlowe
Chief, Technical Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Ms. Wardlowe:

I would first like to express my sincere appreciation for your time and
assistance in talking with me today regarding the Bob Dorr Election
Committee and my questions regarding compliance with the Proposition 73
contribution limits during the Special Election cycle for State Senate
District #1.  I believe it is safe to say that there is some confusion
surrounding the mechanical aspects.

I want to reiterate the Committee's particular circumstances as well as
set forth my understanding of our conversation.  On June 4, 1990, Bob
Dorr filed Form 501, Notice of Intention, for the 1992 election in State
Senate District Number One.  Funds were raised and expenditures made
during 1990 and the required semi-annual reports were filed in July,
1990, and January, 1991.  Subsequent to the vacating of the District 1
Senate seat by John Doolittle, Bob Dorr has filed an amended Form 501
designating his intent to participate in the March, 19, 1991, Special
Election.  An amended Form 502 has also been filed redesignating the
Committee bank account to be utilized for that Special Election.

It is my understanding that as of January 2, 1991, the date the seat
was vacated by Mr. Doolittle, a Special Election cycle began which
constitutes a new fiscal period for purposes of applying the Proposition
73 contribution limitations and that the cycle will end on March 19,
1991, the date of the election.  If a run-off election is required,
another fiscal period will begin on March 20, 1991, and will end upon
the date of that run-off election.  Since the Bob Dorr Committee has
chosen to carry over funds on hand at January 2, 1991, for use during

3377 COACH LANE, SUITE G / P.O. BOX 1070 / CAMERON PARK / CA 95682 / 916-677-0219

Ms. Carla Wardlowe
February 4, 1991
Page 2


the Special Election cycle, it is my understanding that we must
apportion the approximately $46,000 on hand to specific contributors in
order to determine eligibility for future contributions. As you and I
discussed, we propose to make that apportionment on the basis of
assuming funds on hand are attributable to the most recent contributions
received. You suggested that such an apportionment was consistent with
a previous FPPC position regarding the prohibition of carryover funds
from pre-January 1, 1989, contributions (Regulation 18536.1) and,
therefore, would be considered reasonable. We also agreed that the
Committee would provide a supplemental schedule accompanying its first
Pre-Election Statement setting forth the results of that apportionment.

We discussed the inclusion of contributions received subsequent to
December 31, 1990, but prior to January 2, 1991, in the Committee's
first Pre-Election Statement due this week. It was your opinion that
those funds should be included in that filing and the Committee will do
so. The other item discussed was whether any outstanding and unpaid
bills of the Committee existing on January 2, 1991, would need to be
considered in any manner and, I believe the conclusion drawn was that
they are of no importance for purposes of the apportionment.

It is my understanding that verbal advice from your office may not be
considered as "formal advice." I would, therefore, respectfully request
written communication addressing the items discussed in this letter.
Due to the fact that we must file our first Pre-Election Statement this
week, I would appreciate a telephone call if I have misstated anything
from our conversation. I again offer my sincere appreciation for your
assistance.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
VGS:mab

sections.question_synthetic: The sender requests written confirmation of verbal advice regarding how to apportion carry-over campaign funds ($45,000) from a prior election cycle to determine contributor eligibility under Proposition 73 contribution limits for a special election beginning January 2, 1991.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. Sender explicitly states verbal advice may not be considered 'formal advice' and requests written communication. No formal Q/C sections present. Document appears to be a follow-up to a phone consultation with FPPC staff member Carla Wardlowe.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The sender requests written confirmation of verbal advice regarding how to apportion carry-over campaign funds ($45,000) from a prior election cycle to determine contributor eligibility under Proposition 73 contribution limits for a special election beginning January 2, 1991.

I want to reiterate the Committee's particular circumstances as well as set forth my understanding of our conversation. On June 4, 1990, Bob Dorr filed Form 501, Notice of Intention, for the 1992 election in State Senate District Number One. Subsequent to the vacating of the District 1 Senate seat by John Doolittle, Bob Dorr has filed an amended Form 501 designating his intent to participate in the March, 19, 1991, Special Election. It is my understanding that as of January 2, 1991, the date the seat was vacated by Mr. Doolittle, a Special Election cycle began which constitutes a new fiscal period for purposes of applying the Proposition 73 contribution limitations and that the cycle will end on March 19, 1991, the date of the election. As you and I discussed, we propose to make that apportionment on the basis of assuming funds on hand are attributable to the most recent contributions received. We also agreed that the Committee would provide a supplemental schedule accompanying its first Pre-Election Statement setting forth the results of that apportionment. We discussed the inclusion of contributions received subsequent to December 31, 1990, but prior to January 2, 1991, in the Committee's first Pre-Election Statement due this week. It was your opinion that those funds should be included in that filing and the Committee will do so.
embedding.summary: This is a letter from a CPA representing the Bob Dorr Election Committee requesting formal written confirmation of verbal advice previously given by FPPC staff regarding the apportionment of carry-over funds and compliance with Proposition 73 contribution limits for a special election in State Senate District 1.
